Bash(git commit -m "$(cat <<''EOF''
Feature 2: Bulk Operations on Multiple Devices

Implemented parallel command execution across multiple devices:

Backend (modules/bulk_ops.py):
- BulkOperationManager class for coordinating operations
- execute_bulk_command(): Parallel execution with worker threads
- get_operation_status(): Real-time progress tracking
- clear_operation(): Cleanup completed operations
- Thread-safe operation tracking with locks
- Configurable worker pool (default: 5 parallel workers)

Routes (app.py):
- POST /bulk_execute: Start bulk operation on selected devices
- GET /bulk_status/<operation_id>: Poll operation progress
- POST /bulk_clear/<operation_id>: Clear completed operation

UI (templates/index.html):
- Checkbox column for device selection
- "Select All" checkbox in table header
- Bulk Operations panel (hidden until devices selected)
- Command input with Execute/Clear buttons
- Real-time selected device counter
- Results modal with:
  - Live progress bar
  - Success/failure counters
  - Per-device results with collapsible output
  - Auto-polling for updates (1s interval)
  - Auto-stops when operation completes

Features:
✅ Select multiple online devices with checkboxes
✅ Select all/clear all functionality
✅ Parallel execution (up to 5 devices simultaneously)
✅ Real-time progress tracking
✅ Per-device success/failure status
✅ Full command output for each device
✅ Error messages for failed devices
✅ Toast notifications for feedback
✅ Confirmation prompt before execution
✅ Only online devices can be selected

Use Cases:
- Update ACLs on all edge routers
- Check versions across infrastructure
- Apply configuration changes to groups
- Gather information from multiple devices
- Run show commands on entire fleet
